children.heading: Mandatory Reporting by Responsible Entity of a Vessel.—
children.label: a Mandatory Reporting by Responsible Entity of a Vessel.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The responsible entity of a vessel shall report to the Commandant any complaint or incident of sexual harassment or sexual assault in violation of employer policy or law or harassment, of which such entity is made aware.

children.heading: .—Penalty
children.label: 2 .—Penalty
children.text: A responsible entity of a vessel who knowingly fails to report in compliance with paragraph (1) is liable to the United States Government for a civil penalty of not more than $50,000.

children.label: A
children.text: a single entity in the Coast Guard designated by the Commandant to receive such reports; and

children.label: B
children.text: the appropriate officer or agency of the government of the country in whose waters the incident occurs.

children.label: A
children.text: the name, official position or role in relation to the vessel, and contact information of such individual;

children.label: B
children.text: the name and official number of the documented vessel;

children.label: C
children.text: the time and date of the incident;

children.label: D
children.text: the geographic position or location of the vessel when the incident occurred; and

children.label: E
children.text: a brief description of the alleged sexual harassment or sexual assault being reported.

children.label: I
children.text: a single telephone number that is continuously manned at all times; and

children.label: II
children.text: a single email address that is continuously monitored; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall use procedures that include preserving evidence in such reports and providing emergency service referrals.

children.heading: .—Collection of information
children.label: B .—Collection of information
children.text: After receipt of the report made under subsection (a), the Coast Guard shall collect information related to the identity of each alleged victim, alleged perpetrator, and any witnesses identified in the report through means designed to protect, to the extent practicable, the personal identifiable information of such individuals.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Commandant may compel the testimony of witnesses and the production of any evidence by subpoena to determine compliance with this section.

children.heading: .—Jurisdictional limits
children.label: 2 .—Jurisdictional limits
children.text: The jurisdictional limits of a subpoena issued under this section are the same as, and are enforceable in the same manner as, subpoenas issued under chapter 63 of this title.

children.label: A
children.text: submit to the Commandant a document with detailed information to describe the actions taken by such entity after becoming aware of the sexual assault or sexual harassment incident, including the results of any investigation into the complaint or incident and any action taken against the offending individual; and

children.label: B
children.text: make such submission not later than 10 days after such entity made the report under subsection (a).

children.heading: .—Civil penalty
children.label: 2 .—Civil penalty
children.text: A responsible entity of a vessel that fails to comply with paragraph (1) is liable to the United States Government for a civil penalty of $25,000 and $500 shall be added for each day of noncompliance, except that the total amount of a penalty with respect to a complaint or incident shall not exceed $50,000 per violation.

children.heading: .—Investigatory Audit
children.label: e .—Investigatory Audit
children.text: The Commandant shall periodically perform an audit or other systematic review of the submissions made under this section to determine if there were any failures to comply with the requirements of this section.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: 1 .—Regulations
children.text: The Secretary may issue regulations to implement the requirements of this section.

children.heading: .—Interim reports
children.label: 2 .—Interim reports
children.text: Any report required to be made to the Commandant under this section shall be made to the Coast Guard National Command Center, until regulations implementing the procedures required by this section are issued.

children.heading: .—Definition of Responsible Entity of a Vessel
children.label: g .—Definition of Responsible Entity of a Vessel

children.text: the owner, master, or managing operator of a documented vessel engaged in commercial service; or
the employer of a seafarer on such a vessel.

children.label: i
children.text: title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 2000e

children.label: ii
children.text: the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 ( et seq.);29 U.S.C. 621

children.label: iii
children.text: the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 ( et seq.); and42 U.S.C. 12101

children.label: iv
children.text: title II of the Genetic Information Nondisclosure Act ( et seq.); and142 U.S.C. 2000ffSee References in Text note below.1

children.label: i
children.text: submission to such remarks or conduct is made either explicitly or implicitly a term or condition of employment, pay, career, benefits, or entitlements of an individual; or

children.label: ii
children.text: submission to or rejection of such remarks or conduct by an individual is used as the basis for decisions affecting that individual’s job, pay, career, benefits, or entitlements; or

children.label: iii
children.text: such remarks or conduct have the purpose or effect of unreasonably interfering with an individual’s work performance; and

children.label: C
children.text: such remarks or conduct are so severe or pervasive that a reasonable person would perceive, and the alleged harassed individual does perceive, the environment as hostile or offensive.

children.heading: .—Response to Incidents
children.label: i .—Response to Incidents
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to impede the ability of the responsible entity of the vessel to take immediate personnel action in response to an incident described in subsection (a)(1) to preserve the safety and security of individuals on the vessel.

children.label: i
children.text: the purpose, functions, and powers of the Commission;

children.label: ii
children.text: the role of the Commission in addressing employment discrimination complaints; and

children.label: B
children.text: identify the publicly available websites and contact information for the Commission; and
make available trainings or other presentations to inform seafarers of employment and anti-discrimination rights under the laws administered by the Coast Guard and the Commission.
